tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: From Football's Shadow to Glory: Defender Canale Leads Paraguay Past Germany
stories.summary: Jose Canale, a 30-year-old defender who bounced between clubs for years, scored the decisive penalty on his first competitive start to knock four-time champion Germany out of the World Cup.

stories.headline: Couple Stays Behind in Wildfire to Care for 51 Abandoned Dogs
stories.summary: During evacuation, Diedra and Derek remained to feed 51 stranded dogs three times daily, bringing solace to families who feared for their pets.

stories.headline: Black Skimmer Chicks Hatch on Florida Beach After 30 Years of Silence
stories.summary: After 30 years of silence, the first black skimmer chick has hatched on Sanibel Causeway, where volunteers are painting shelters to protect the fragile nesting colony.

stories.headline: Mozambique's Rhino Rescue: 61 Animals Settle Into New Home, Calf Already Born
stories.summary: Mozambique returned 61 rhinos to Zinave National Park after a devastating civil war wiped them out, and a black rhino calf has already been born.

stories.headline: Indian Surgeons Gift Engineer Second Life with Landmark Five-Organ Transplant
stories.summary: Indian surgeons performed a grueling 36-hour surgery to transplant five organs—stomach, pancreas, small intestine, duodenum, and colon—into a 30-year-old engineer, India's first multi-visceral transplant.

stories.headline: Washoe Tribe and Federal Partners Restore Ancient Trout to Lake Tahoe
stories.summary: The Washoe Tribe and U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service together released over 1,000 native Lahontan cutthroat trout into Lake Tahoe, restoring a culturally vital species after decades.

stories.headline: Windsor Community Delivers Comfort Pizza to Cancer Fighter in Northern Ontario
stories.summary: A Facebook craving became an outpouring of love when a community sent special pizza to support an evacuee's cancer-stricken father.

stories.headline: Champion Badminton Star Loses Medal Match, Wins Hearts with Honest Sportsmanship
stories.summary: Srikanth intentionally forfeited a point to correct a referee's mistake, proving character and integrity matter more than trophies.

stories.headline: Lost Moth Found: Dover Twist Returns After 73 Years Missing From England
stories.summary: The Dover Twist moth, a tiny 16mm-winged species missing for 73 years, is thriving again after ecologist Rebecca Levey rediscovered it last year and over 20 were found in a recent survey.

stories.headline: Florist Leaves 20 Free Bouquets Across Town to Brighten Winter Days
stories.summary: Sarah Lang and her employee left 20 free flower bouquets around Launceston for strangers to find and brighten their winter days.

stories.headline: Road Patcher's 70 Years of Quiet Service Honored by Grateful Community
stories.summary: Chee Siew Kuan, a 70-year-old who has spent nearly a decade patching potholes with construction debris and cleaning roads, finally received recognition and aid from his grateful community.

stories.headline: Scientists Build Living Cell From Scratch, Opening Doors to Life's Mysteries
stories.summary: Researchers created a fully synthetic cell that grows, replicates DNA, and divides—a landmark breakthrough toward understanding life itself.
